import os, glob, textwrap, json, re, sys, unicodedata, itertools, pprint, math, statistics

# Let's list files in /mnt/data to find the file path
import os, glob, json, re
files = glob.glob("/mnt/data/*")

path = "/mnt/data/file-NZLBu8sY7TX9PwD6rQcs9k-text_to_analyse.txt"
with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    text = f.read()
len(text)

text = open("/mnt/data/file-NZLBu8sY7TX9PwD6rQcs9k-text_to_analyse.txt","r",encoding="utf-8").read()
# Find quoted segments inside double quotes
quotes = []
idx = 0
while True:
    i = text.find('"', idx)
    if i == -1:
        break
    j = text.find('"', i+1)
    if j == -1:
        break
    quotes.append((i, j))
    idx = j+1

quotes, [text[i+1:j] for (i,j) in quotes]

# find all parentheses segments starting with '(' and containing Arabic letters
import re
open_indices = [m.start() for m in re.finditer(r'\(', text)]
open_indices, len(open_indices)

# For each opening '(', find the closing ')'; if not found, find end of line before 'References:'
segments = []
for i in open_indices:
    j = text.find(')', i+1)
    if j != -1:
        seg = text[i+1:j]
        segments.append((i, j, seg))
    else:
        # take until end of line or next non-Arabic segment
        # Let's define end at the end of the line where 'References:' occurs.
        # We'll take until the end of the line (i.e., until '\n' after i).
        nl = text.find('\n', i+1)
        if nl == -1:
            nl = len(text)
        seg = text[i+1:nl]
        segments.append((i, nl, seg))
segments

queries: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ إِنَّا خَلَقْنَاكُمْ مِنْ ذَكَرٍ وَأُنْثَى وَجَعَلْنَاكُمْ شُعُوبًا وَقَبَائِلَ لِتَعَارَفُوا إِنَّ أَكْرَمَكُمْ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ أَتْقَاكُمْ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَلِيمٌ خَبِيرٌ
وَلَهُنَّ مِثْلُ الَّذِي عَلَيْهِنَّ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَلِلرِّجَالِ عَلَيْهِنَّ دَرَجَةٌ
وَقُلْنَا يَا آدَمُ اسْكُنْ أَنْتَ وَزَوْجُكَ الْجَنَّةَ وَكُلا مِنْهَا رَغَداً حَيْثُ شِئْتُمَا وَلا تَقْرَبَا هَذِهِ الشَّجَرَةَ فَتَكُونَا مِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ
إنما النساء شقائق الرجال أبو داود
وقلنا يا آدم اسكن أنت وزوجك الجنة

# Verify that span_text matches the actual slice
text_data = open("/mnt/data/file-NZLBu8sY7TX9PwD6rQcs9k-text_to_analyse.txt", "r", encoding="utf-8").read()
for idx, c in enumerate(obj["citations"]):
    s, e = c["span_start"], c["span_end"]
    actual = text_data[s:e]
    assert actual == c["span_text"], f"Mismatch at citation {idx}: {actual!r} vs expected {c['span_text']!r}"
"OK"

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
